chapter_title.en: The Rules of Forgetfulness (sahw) in Prayer

text: 990 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اِبْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ أَنَّ اَلصَّادِقَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «إِذَا كَانَ اَلرَّجُلُ مِمَّنْ يَسْهُو فِي كُلِّ ثَلاَثٍ فَهُوَ مِمَّنْ كَثُرَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّهْوُ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.990 - In the narration of Ibn Abi Umayr, from Muhammad ibn Abi Hamza said:
"The Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: 'If a man is one who forgets or becomes distracted in every third part of his prayer, then he is among those for whom distractions are frequent.'"
